//includeTags: We generate ONLY the routes a screen actually runs through. That is not scope discipline -- it is the
only thing that makes src/app/api/** trustworthy.
THE RULE: a route joins this list when, and only when, its C# gains a .Produces<T>(). Annotate first,
then generate. NEVER widen this list to 'fix' a missing method.
Why: an unannotated route returns `IResult` via `Results.Ok(x)`, and ApiExplorer CANNOT infer a schema
from that -- OpenAPI describes it as '200: OK' with no content. It also carries the default assembly tag
'TimesheetApp.Api', which is not in this list, so it is simply omitted. Add its tag WITHOUT annotating it
and you get a method typed `void` for an endpoint that in fact returns data: a client that LIES,
committed to the repo, that a later wave would reasonably trust.
ADMIN-GATED ROUTES ARE IN THE CLIENT ON PURPOSE, AND THE GUARD MOVED (M9 P2, 2026-07-13).
Until M9 there was no admin-only SCREEN, so an admin route in the client could only ever 403 -- a
correctly-typed client that fails for every ordinary user. /api/users/all and /api/pca-contacts/all
were therefore kept OUT, enforced by a contract test.
M9 CREATES the Users and Settings screens, which MUST list DEACTIVATED users and contacts (USR-01;
'Activate' is impossible without them), and only the /all routes carry those. So ~30 admin routes are
now generated, deliberately. The old test was deleted -- it asserted a PROXY for the security property
(the route is absent from the client). Two stronger guards replaced it, asserting the property itself:
- API:    OpenApiContractTests -- the /all routes 403 for a NON-admin (seeded non-admin, deliberately)
- CLIENT: core/admin.guard.ts  -- /users and /settings are unreachable for a non-admin, and the
sidebar hides them.
🔴 SO: a screen a NON-ADMIN can reach must NEVER call an admin route. That is now the whole contract.
Check .RequireAuthorization(AuthSetup.AdminPolicy) before you call a generated method from a screen.
`void` is NOT automatically a red flag. As of M9, 24 generated functions are legitimately void -- 21
declare 204 via the NON-GENERIC .Produces(StatusCodes.Status204NoContent) overload (which a 'grep for
.Produces<' mental model cannot see), opsRetentionRun is 202 Accepted, smart-fill validate is a
deliberately empty 200, and logout just clears the cookie. The invariant is not 'no void'. It is:
VOID ONLY WHERE THE ROUTE GENUINELY HAS NO BODY. A void for a route that returns data is the bug.
🔴 A QUERY PARAM THE HANDLER READS OFF HttpContext IS INVISIBLE TO ApiExplorer, AND SO TO THIS CLIENT.
EffectiveTeamIds() reads `teamIds` from HttpContext.Request.Query BY HAND -- deliberately, because a
bound `[FromQuery] int[]?` cannot tell 'key absent' from 'key present but empty' (both bind to an EMPTY
ARRAY, never null) while `null` means EVERY TEAM to the repository. That hand-read is a data-leak guard
and must stay. But it also means the generator never sees the param. Any route that wants a client-side
team filter must ALSO declare a (possibly unused) `[FromQuery] int[]? teamIds` so ApiExplorer emits it.
Counts drift as screens land -- do not treat them as an invariant. As of M9: 18 tags, 87 operations,
86 models. Export (excel + markdown) is deliberately EXCLUDED: those routes return binary/text, not
JSON, so the client hand-writes a blob download instead.
//ignoreUnusedModels: Keeps the emitted model set to what the included operations actually reference.
